title: Treasury Quarterly Refunding Process
summary: This page describes Treasury's quarterly refunding process, which involves soliciting input from primary dealers and the Treasury Borrowing Advisory Committee (TBAC) to inform debt management decisions. The process includes face-to-face meetings with half of primary dealers each quarter, formal TBAC reports to the Secretary, and public release of meeting minutes the day after meetings. Specific policy changes are typically announced through quarterly refunding statements but may also appear in separate press releases or Treasury official speeches.
key_points: Treasury solicits advice from primary dealers through questionnaires posted on Treasury.gov and holds face-to-face meetings with half of primary dealers each quarter
The Treasury Borrowing Advisory Committee (TBAC) provides formal advice in response to Treasury questions and discussion materials, issuing a report to the Secretary of the Treasury
TBAC meeting minutes are released to the public the day following the meeting
Debt management policy changes are usually announced through quarterly refunding statements but may also appear in separate press releases or Treasury official speeches
Treasury accepts public input on debt management policy via debt.management@treasury.gov
Specific changes to debt management policy are based on Treasury analyses combined with private sector advice
why_it_matters: This page describes the procedural framework for Treasury's quarterly refunding process, which is a key mechanism for Treasury borrowing and debt issuance decisions. While this page does not contain specific issuance amounts, auction schedules, or policy changes, it provides context on how Treasury incorporates market participant input into debt management decisions—a process that can influence Treasury market dynamics and broader macro conditions.
macro_context: The quarterly refunding process is a regular mechanism through which Treasury makes borrowing decisions that affect the supply of Treasuries in the market. These decisions have macro implications for interest rates, market liquidity, and government financing costs. However, this informational page contains no specific data or policy changes to assess macro impact.
market_structure_context: This page describes the institutional process for Treasury debt management consultations. Primary dealers play a key role in Treasury market-making and the quarterly refunding process. TBAC provides formal advice that informs debt management policy. Meeting minutes are released publicly the day after meetings.
